kills: FATAL TO THE FLAGSHIP EXPERIMENT: S199 is a chi-4 graph, not 'the smallest lineage chi-5 graph'. The repo's own SAT measurement (experiments/LEARNINGS.md lines 590-598, h6 small-gadget triage: S199 199 vtx, 888 edges, chi = 4, omega <= 3; same n/edges as _cache/shotD/S199.summary.json) contradicts the proposal's base-graph choice. L403 and T721 are also chi = 4. The '12 chi-5 UDGs (199-874)' premise inherited from L56/L57 wording is wrong for 3 of the 12; the chi-5 members are 510/517/529/553/610/633/803/826/874. Stages 2-3 on S199 probe 5-colorings of a 4-chromatic graph, where a full spare color makes joint forcing near-impossible; the experiment as specified is close to vacuous. (Independent sanity check: a 199-vtx chi-5 UDG would smash the published ~510-vtx world record.)

C1's diagonal SAT screen is degenerate as designed: any vertex at radius 1/(2 sin(theta/2)) from the center O gives a self-pair (v,v) in R(rho), i.e., the clause x_v != x_v, trivially UNSAT with zero chromatic content. The lineage graphs are built on rings cocircular about the origin, so the top-multiplicity census angles are exactly these ring self-resonances; at the richest known closing angle (rot60, self-pair radius exactly 1) the graphs have many radius-1 vertices. The stated decision rule 'any Stage 2 UNSAT = breakthrough candidate, escalate to VERIFIER' fires on garbage. Fixable (exclude u = v), but unfixed it invalidates the screen and its 'prediction: at least one UNSAT'.

C4's 'theorem part' is false as stated, twice. (a) It omits a distinctness condition: copies j, j' coincide whenever N divides (j-j')(m+1), so gcd(m+1, N) = 1 is required. Concrete counterexample: m = 6, N = 7 gives theta = 2pi*7/42 = pi/3, the symmetry rotation itself; every 'copy' is copy 0 relabeled and Z degenerates to G (5-colorable), while the band-1 chain criterion claims otherwise. (b) The 'Z is 5-colorable iff band-1 twisted chain exists' ignores higher bands; the repo's own h7b data (LEARNINGS lines 481-484) shows band-2 DOMINATES band-1 at the relevant resonance (b_02 = 5001 > b_01 = 4378 for rot60 on P_510). Only the one-sided direction (no band-1 chain implies Z not 5-colorable) survives.

The claimed transport-period machinery does not exist: L55's 'transfer-matrix code path' (wildcard_b_rg_transfer.py) is exact boolean algebra on k x k SINGLE-vertex-port relations. Wide-interface boundary-class enumeration over 10-60 boundary vertices is unwritten code, and L57's universal-freeness implies the feasible boundary-coloring count is plausibly 10^8+, far beyond blocking-clause enumeration. The '~1 hour for three transport-period computations' estimate is not credible; Stage 3's period diagnostic is feasible only for tiny active boundaries, exactly the regime where transport is too permissive to be periodic.

Plausibility is materially oversold against the repo's own data: at the only closing resonance known to be cross-rich (rot60 about origin on P_510), the 3-copy orbit with 13757 exact cross edges was 5-colored by cadical in 0.06-0.13 seconds (LEARNINGS line 483). A transport relation that permissive almost certainly has diagonal fixed points and period 1, which kills C2's grading at that resonance before any necklace is built. Combined with L57 flexibility and the no-buffer prior, the 'why plausible' analogies (hex 7-coloring Z/7 grading, Polymath phase anecdotes) carry no weight: the hex grading certifies an upper bound on a DIFFERENT coloring set.

Stage mismatch in the program's architecture: C1 screens the top-100 resonance angles, which are generically irrational-cosine algebraic angles where the orbit NEVER closes; C2/C4 need closing angles (rational multiples of 2pi, cosine in cyclotomic fields), where resonances exist only by Diophantine coincidence in the coordinate ring (rot60-type angles for this hex-derived lineage) or by tuning O on per-pair center circles, which yields only ~2 deliberate coincidences plus symmetry orbits. A C1 UNSAT at a non-closing angle feeds nothing downstream; the proposal never reconciles the two angle regimes.
prior_art_collisions: Exoo-Ismailescu small 5-chromatic UDG constructions: unions of rotated copies at exact binding angles (rotation-linkage rings) are their core technique, so the 'resonance census + rotated union' half of the proposal is established art; only the identified-coloring diagonal and the closed/twisted necklace are new.

de Grey 2018: the rotated-copy union mechanism and the monochromatic-pair lemma at 4 colors (acknowledged by the proposal); Polymath16 ran the forced-monochromatic-distance program at 5 colors within graphs and failed, which is adjacent prior art for C1's cross-rotation variant.

Fisk's geometric coloring theory (1970s): monodromy of 4-colorings of triangulations is genuine prior art for the 'coloring monodromy' concept; never applied to UDGs, but the novelty claim 'appears nowhere I know of' should cite it.

Height-function / square-ice theory: Z-valued windings on 3-colorings of planar lattices are the established 'winding number on a coloring set' phenomenon; C2's Z/m grading is its discrete cousin and should be calibrated against it (height functions exist because of very special lattice structure, a warning sign for generic UDGs).

Repo-internal: h7/h7b (open rotated unions of P_510, all 5-colorable up to 13757 bridges) and e1e (binding-rotation enumeration on the Moser spindle) cover the resonance supply; L55 (imprimitivity diagnostic) and L56/L57 (single-pair forcing exhaustively dead) define what the proposal must and does escape.
Heule cnp-sat / Parts minimization corpus supplies all 12 graphs; note S199/L403/T721 in that corpus are chi-4 gadgets, not chi-5 records.
result.surviving_kernel: The closed (optionally sigma-twisted) rotation necklace as a born-realized chi>=6 certificate candidate, plus the identified-coloring diagonal instance as its cheap necessary-condition screen. This kernel genuinely survives every logged kill: it is W3-free by construction (the certificate is an explicit algebraic point set), it is L55 route (ii) (wide interface) rather than the dead monoid route, and L56/L57 kill only single-pair forcing while necklace UNSAT needs only joint boundary rigidity up to recoloring, which is strictly weaker per pair. C2's consequence logic is a valid theorem schema (a Z/m grading advanced by every band-1 arc forces Z_N UNSAT for m not dividing N; any proper coloring of Z_N gives a closed N-walk in the class digraph, and higher-band edges only strengthen UNSAT). C3 (equivariant 5-colorability) is a cheap, genuinely uncomputed invariant, best aimed at the symmetric ancestors (de Grey 1581/1585 core via e1l) rather than the SAT-minimized 12, whose geometric automorphism groups are likely trivial. The expected outcome remains negative (fixed points and period 1 everywhere), but the negative is decisive, cheap, and new.
result.cheapest_decisive_test: Build Z_6(P_510, origin, rot60): the union of rho_{pi/3}^j(P_510), j = 0..5, with exact sympy unit-edge detection across ALL bands (~3060 vtx; reuse h7b_orbit_coupling.py code paths, which already verified the 3-copy case with b_01 = 4378, b_02 = 5001), then one cadical 5-SAT call with glucose dual-confirmation. h7b's 0.06-0.13 s solve at 3 copies says this resolves in minutes. SAT (overwhelmingly likely) kills C2/C4 at the only known cross-rich closing resonance and demotes the program to armature engineering; UNSAT is a chi(R^2) >= 6 certificate outright. Run alongside it the sub-second corrected diagonal screen at rot60 (one variable set, self-pairs u = v excluded): SAT there additionally exhibits the transport fixed point that C2's grading forbids.
result.steelman: Drop S199 and all chi-4 gadgets; rebase everything on P_510 (and 517-874) about the origin, where rot60 is PROVEN cross-rich (4378 band-1, 5001 band-2 exact bridges). Reorder the program around closure from the start: (1) enumerate closing angles only (theta = 2pi k/N with cos theta in the coordinate field, plus the C4 twisted family with gcd(m+1, N) = 1 fixed), and for each, count exact cross pairs per band, tuning the center O along per-pair center circles to stack coincidences; (2) run the diagonal screen with self-pairs excluded AND the full S_5 quasi-fixed-point variant (c compose sigma = pi compose c for each cycle type pi, a few dozen sub-second SAT calls), since plain-diagonal UNSAT is too weak to feed closure; (3) SAT the full closed necklaces Z_N directly (all bands, exact edges), starting with Z_6(P_510, rot60), rather than trusting any band-1 transfer analysis; (4) restate C4 correctly as one-sided (band-1 twisted-chain UNSAT implies Z UNSAT) with the coprimality condition; (5) treat the period diagnostic as a luxury, attempted only at resonances whose active boundary has at most ~10 vertices, with honest acknowledgment that no existing repo code does wide-interface enumeration; (6) aim C3 at de Grey 1581/1585 (real symmetry groups) and log equivariant 5-colorability as a standalone structural datum. The honest framing: this is a half-day falsification of the monodromy hypothesis on the lineage, whose all-SAT outcome cleanly redirects to flexible armatures or new cores, and whose any-UNSAT outcome is the prize; either result is worth logging, but the prior is heavily on SAT.
